per_probe.note: Qualitative only: inspect whether noise is narrativized into objects (not auto-scored)

answers.llava_ov: The image is a digital representation of what appears to be static or noise, commonly seen on analog television screens when no signal is received. It consists of a multitude of small, multicolored dots that create a pattern resembling the grainy texture one might associate with old film photography or the visual interference experienced on older TV sets. The colors vary across the spectrum, including shades of red, green, blue, and yellow, among others, giving it a somewhat pixelated appearance.
